title: Judges: Decline and Deliverers
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of judges: decline and deliverers and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study judges: decline and deliverers and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was the source of Samson's extraordinary strength according to Judges? Answer: his long hair, as a Nazirite consecrated to God from birth.
segments.correct_answer: his uncut hair as a Nazirite set apart to God from birth
acceptable_variants: long hair as a symbol of his nazirite vow
his strength came from his consecration to God — symbolized by his hair
samson's hair was the outward sign of his nazirite dedication

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: One who takes a vow of consecration in Numbers 6, abstaining from wine, not cutting hair, and avoiding corpses; Samson's Nazirite status from birth made his strength a divine gift, not a personal power
The Philistine woman who discovered the source of Samson's strength and betrayed him to the Philistines; Samson's failure with her is the final stage of his compromised Nazirite life
